results.rendered_html:
agādha (3. a + gādha)
1〉 adj. f. ā  R. 5,72,12 . unergründlich, überaus tief  AK. 1,2,3,15 .  H. 1070 . an. 3,341 .  MED. dh. 27 . tatsvabhāvo mamāpyeṣa yadagādho 'vyayaḥ . vikārastu bhavedgādha iti tatte vadāmyaham (das Meer) ..  R. 5,94,11 . parikhābhiḥ — agādhāvyayatoyābhiḥ 5,9,15 . agādhe vipule hrade  N. 6,12 . śoṇaḥ śubhajalo 'gādhaḥ  R. 1,36,4 . agādhasalilātsamudrāt  HIT. I, 46 . übertr.: sāsmyagādhe bhaye magnā 2,7,20 . agādhabuddhi  MBH. 18,157 . agādhasattva  MṚCCH. 1,13 .  MAHĀV. 93, ult. 
— 2〉 m. Loch in der Erde  H. 1364 . an. 3,341 . n.  MED. dh. 27 .
— 3〉 m. Name eines der fünf Feuer beim svadhākāra  HARIV. 10467 .
— Vgl. āgādha.

results.rendered_html:
agādha a. [gādh-pratiṣṭhāyāṃ ghañ na. ba.] Unfathomable, very deep, bottomless; agādhasalilātsamudrāt  H. 1. 52 ; (fig.) profound, sound, very deep, ˚satva  Mk. 1. 3 ,  R. 6. 21 ; so ˚jñānaṃ, ˚buddhi; great; as ˚bhayaṃ; yasya jñānadayāsiṃdhoragādhasyānaghā guṇāḥ  Ak.  unfathomable, incomprehensible, inscrutable.
— dhaḥ
— dhaṃ a deep hole or chasm.
— dhaḥ N. of one of the 5 fires at the svāhākāra [cf. Gr. agathos].
Comp.
— jalaḥ [agādhaṃ jalaṃ yatra] a deep pool or pond, deep lake.
